site.title: Raino — Agentic PCB & PCBA Workflow Platform
site.description: Constrained, auditable, source-traceable hardware design and manufacturing handoff system

footer.tagline: Agentic PCB & PCBA workflow platform. Constrained, auditable, source-traceable hardware design.
footer.kicadDisclaimer: KiCad is a trademark of the KiCad Project. Not affiliated.

hero.title: Design PCBs with {accent}
hero.description: Raino converts fuzzy hardware intent into structured specs, selects approved architectures, and generates KiCad projects with full audit trails.

stats.architectureTemplatesDesc: Approved templates with formal sufficiency gates

features.title: Everything You Need to {accent}
features.description: From natural language to manufacturing handoff. Raino handles the entire workflow with constrained agents and formal verification.

intake.title: Natural Language Intake
intake.description: Describe hardware in plain language. Raino understands your intent and asks clarifying questions to build a complete specification.

specs.title: Structured Specifications
specs.description: Fuzzy intent becomes precise specs. Every requirement is captured, validated, and traced through the entire workflow.

architectures.title: Approved Architectures
architectures.description: Constrained architecture selection ensures designs stay within validated templates. No unconstrained autopilot.

bom.title: Intelligent BOM
bom.description: Full bill of materials with sourcing data, alternates, and risk indicators. Every part traceable to its source.

kicad.title: KiCad Generation
kicad.description: Automated schematic and PCB generation. Raino produces production-ready KiCad projects, not suggestions.

handoff.title: Manufacturing Handoff
handoff.description: Previews, downloads, rough quotes, and optional PCBA handoff. Everything you need to go from design to production.

testimonials.title: Loved by {accent}
testimonials.description: Teams using Raino report faster iteration, fewer schematic errors, and clearer communication between hardware and software engineers.
testimonials.quote1: Raino cut our early-stage schematic time from two weeks to three days. The structured spec meant our senior engineer reviewed intent, not pinouts.
testimonials.quote2: The BOM with real alternates saved us during the 2023 chip shortage. Raino had already flagged lifecycle risks before we even ordered.
testimonials.quote3: I described a motor driver in a paragraph and got a KiCad project with ERC passing. The clarifying questions caught a voltage level mismatch I would have missed.

integrations.title: Powered By {accent}
integrations.description: Raino integrates with industry-standard suppliers, databases, and design tools. Every connection has honest fallback modes.

architecture.title: System {accent}
architecture.description: Raino is built as a modular system with clear boundaries. The marketing site and product studio communicate with worker services through well-defined APIs.
architecture.marketingSite: Marketing site — Public-facing information and documentation
architecture.corePackages: Core packages — Schemas, validation, domain logic, RAG, LLM gateway
architecture.workerServices: Worker services — Ingest, design, quote, audit
architecture.externalBoundaries: External boundaries — Supabase, KiCad CLI, supplier APIs

howItWorks.title: How It {accent}
howItWorks.description: A 12-step workflow from natural language to manufacturing handoff. Every step has formal validation and audit trails.
howItWorks.step1Desc: Describe your hardware in plain language
howItWorks.step4Desc: Approved template matched to your needs
howItWorks.step6Desc: Datasheets, errata, and app notes analyzed
howItWorks.step7Desc: Real prices, stock, and MOQs from suppliers
howItWorks.step9Desc: Full BOM with alternates and risk indicators

openSource.title: Open {accent}
openSource.description: Raino is MIT licensed. The entire codebase is available on GitHub. Contribute, fork, or deploy your own instance.

hero.title: Features
hero.description: Every capability in Raino is designed for production hardware workflows. No gimmicks. No autopilot. Just structured engineering.

cta.title: Ready to build something?
cta.description: Raino guides you from a description to manufacturing-ready outputs. Every step is constrained, traceable, and production-focused.

feature1.title: Natural Language Intake
feature1.subtitle: Describe hardware in plain language
feature1.description: Start with a simple description of what you want to build. Raino parses natural language input to extract hardware requirements, pin constraints, power budgets, and connectivity needs.
feature1.useCase: A hardware engineer describes a sensor board with 5V input, I2C interface, and specific dimensions. Raino asks clarifying questions about sensor type, sampling rate, and environmental constraints.

feature2.title: Structured Specifications
feature2.subtitle: Fuzzy intent becomes precise specs
feature2.description: Every requirement is captured in a structured format with validation rules. Specifications are versioned, traceable, and linked to their source rationale.
feature2.useCase: The engineer reviews the structured specification generated from their description. Each requirement shows its source (which part of the original description) and confidence score.

feature3.title: Approved Architectures
feature3.subtitle: Constrained architecture selection
feature3.description: Raino does not generate unconstrained designs. Instead, it matches your requirements against a library of pre-validated architecture templates.
feature3.useCase: Based on the sensor board requirements, Raino recommends the "Low-Power Sensor Node" template, which includes validated power management and communication subcircuits.

feature4.title: Intelligent BOM
feature4.subtitle: Full bill of materials with sourcing
feature4.description: The BOM includes every component with procurement data from real suppliers, alternates for each line item, and risk indicators for availability and lifecycle.

capabilities.cap1: Real supplier price queries (DigiKey, Mouser, JLCPCB)
capabilities.cap2: Alternate part suggestions with compatibility checks

feature4.useCase: The BOM shows the recommended accelerometer with prices from three suppliers, two alternates with trade-off analysis, and a warning that one alternate is not recommended for new designs.

feature5.title: KiCad Generation
feature5.subtitle: Automated schematic and PCB generation
feature5.description: Raino generates production-ready KiCad projects including schematic sheets, PCB layout, design rules, and manufacturing outputs.

capabilities.cap2: PCB layout with design rule checking

feature5.useCase: After reviewing the BOM, Raino generates the KiCad project. The schematic shows the sensor interface circuit, power regulation, and microcontroller connections. The PCB layout respects the dimensional constraints.

feature6.title: Manufacturing Handoff
feature6.subtitle: From design to production
feature6.description: Generate manufacturing bundles with previews, rough quotes, and optional handoff to PCBA services.
feature6.useCase: The engineer reviews the 3D preview of the assembled board, downloads the manufacturing bundle, and gets a rough quote of $45-65 per unit at 100 quantity with medium confidence.

hero.title: System {accent}
hero.description: Raino is built as a modular system with clear boundaries. Each component has a defined responsibility and explicit interfaces.

packages.title: Package {accent}
packages.rainoCore: Zod schemas, validation, quote engine, domain logic
packages.rainoDb: Prisma ORM, Supabase auth, storage, and pgvector clients
packages.rainoLlm: Kimi K2.5 gateway, structured output, retry logic
packages.rainoRag: Chunking, embeddings, retrieval for engineering documents
packages.rainoUi: Shared React components, liquid glass design system
packages.rainoKicadWorkerClient: KiCad CLI contracts and job types
packages.rainoSupplierClients: DigiKey, Mouser, JLCPCB adapter interfaces + factory

ingestWorker.name: ingest-worker
ingestWorker.description: 8-stage document ingestion pipeline for datasheets, errata, and application notes

designWorker.name: design-worker
designWorker.description: KiCad project generation from approved architecture templates and BOM

quoteWorker.name: quote-worker
quoteWorker.description: Rough quote calculation with confidence bands and assumption tracking

auditWorker.name: audit-worker
auditWorker.description: Traces, manifests, provenance tracking for every design decision

kicad.name: KiCad (GPL Boundary)
kicad.description: KiCad is an external GPL-licensed EDA tool. Raino communicates through CLI commands only. No KiCad source code is embedded.
kicad.detail3: Generated designs have separate license considerations

suppliers.name: Supplier Adapters
suppliers.description: Interface + Adapter pattern for DigiKey, Mouser, and JLCPCB. No live credentials assumed.

rag.name: RAG System
rag.description: Retrieval-augmented generation for engineering knowledge. NOT for live pricing or stock data.

ragScope.title: RAG {accent}
ragScope.quoteNote: Quote source of truth is always structured supplier adapter output, never RAG retrieval.

hero.title: The {accent}
hero.description: From natural language to manufacturing handoff. Twelve structured steps with formal validation and full audit trails.

step1.title: Natural Language Intake
step1.description: Describe your hardware in plain language. Raino parses intent, constraints, and requirements from your input.

step2.title: Clarifying Questions
step2.description: Raino resolves ambiguities through a multi-turn question loop before proceeding to formal specs.

step3.title: Structured Specification
step3.description: Fuzzy intent becomes a formal, validated specification with traceability to every requirement.

step4.title: Architecture Selection
step4.description: Your requirements are matched against a library of pre-validated architecture templates.

step5.title: Part Family Selection
step5.description: Candidate components are selected with sourcing data, alternates, and risk indicators.

step6.title: Document Ingestion
step6.description: Datasheets, errata, and application notes are fetched, parsed, chunked, and embedded.

step7.title: Supplier Metadata Resolution
step7.description: Real supplier adapters query DigiKey, Mouser, and JLCPCB for prices, stock, and MOQs.

step8.title: RAG-Assisted Reasoning
step8.description: Engineering knowledge is retrieved from documents to inform component and design decisions.

step9.title: BOM Generation
step9.description: A full KiCad-ready bill of materials is generated with alternates and confidence scoring.

step10.title: KiCad Project Generation
step10.description: Production-ready schematic and PCB files are generated from the approved architecture.

step11.title: Preview & Download
step11.description: Interactive previews, manufacturing bundles, and downloadable artifacts are produced.

step12.title: Quote & Handoff
step12.description: Receive a rough quote with confidence bands and optionally request a PCBA handoff.

cta.title: Ready to {accent}?
cta.description: Launch the Raino studio and run through the workflow with your own hardware idea.

hero.title: {accent}
hero.description: Raino connects to the tools you already use. Every integration has a clear contract, honest fallback modes, and no silent degraded behavior.

philosophy.title: Integration {accent}
philosophy.description: Raino never pretends a live connection exists when it is using fixture data. Every degraded-mode path is inspectable through the audit trail. Mock adapters are permanent, honest fallbacks — not temporary scaffolding.

clearContracts.title: Clear Contracts
clearContracts.description: Every adapter implements a strict interface. Inputs and outputs are Zod-validated.

honestFallbacks.title: Honest Fallbacks
honestFallbacks.description: When credentials are missing, the system falls back to fixture data and says so.

auditEverything.title: Audit Everything
auditEverything.description: Every adapter call, every fallback, every error is logged and inspectable.

kimiK25.name: Kimi K2.5
kimiK25.description: Raino uses Kimi K2.5 via the OpenAI-compatible API at moonshot.ai for natural language intake, clarifying questions, and structured reasoning. Every LLM call includes Zod-validated structured output and exponential backoff retry.
kimiK25.fallback: LLM calls fail gracefully with user-visible error messages
kimiK25.cap4: Structured JSON output with Zod validation

supabase.name: Supabase
supabase.description: Supabase provides Postgres with Row-Level Security, magic-link authentication, pgvector for embeddings, and storage for generated artifacts. Prisma ORM coexists with Supabase RLS for type-safe database access.
supabase.fallback: Degraded mode: no persistence, auth prompts shown
supabase.cap4: File storage for KiCad projects and exports

digikey.name: DigiKey
digikey.description: The DigiKey adapter queries real-time pricing, stock levels, and minimum order quantities through the DigiKey API. Every response includes source attribution and confidence scoring.
digikey.fallback: Fixture mode with labeled estimates from historical data

mouser.name: Mouser
mouser.description: The Mouser adapter provides cross-referenced pricing and availability data. When both DigiKey and Mouser are configured, Raino selects the best source per component and flags discrepancies.
mouser.fallback: Fixture mode with labeled estimates from historical data

jlcpcb.name: JLCPCB
jlcpcb.description: The JLCPCB adapter queries PCB fabrication pricing, assembly costs, and component availability from the JLCPCB parts library. Supports both bare PCB and PCBA quote requests.
jlcpcb.fallback: Fixture mode with labeled estimates from historical data

kicad.name: KiCad
kicad.description: KiCad is an external GPL-licensed EDA tool. Raino communicates through the KiCad CLI only. No KiCad source code is embedded. Generated projects include schematics, PCB layouts, design rules, and manufacturing exports.
kicad.fallback: KiCad generation skipped; user notified with clear message

hero.description: Real hardware projects generated from natural language descriptions. Each one passed ERC/DRC and shipped with a manufacturing-ready bundle.

testimonials.title: Loved by {accent}
testimonials.description: Teams using Raino report faster iteration, fewer schematic errors, and clearer communication between hardware and software engineers.
testimonials.quote1: Raino cut our early-stage schematic time from two weeks to three days. The structured spec meant our senior engineer reviewed intent, not pinouts.
testimonials.quote2: The BOM with real alternates saved us during the 2023 chip shortage. Raino had already flagged lifecycle risks before we even ordered.
testimonials.quote3: I described a motor driver in a paragraph and got a KiCad project with ERC passing. The clarifying questions caught a voltage level mismatch I would have missed.

cta.title: Ready to Build {accent}?
cta.description: Describe your hardware idea in plain language and let Raino generate a structured spec, KiCad project, and manufacturing bundle.

project1.title: Low-Power Sensor Node
project1.description: A battery-operated sensor board with temperature, humidity, and air quality sensing. Raino generated the full KiCad project from a plain-language description including power budget and communication constraints.

project2.title: BLDC Motor Driver
project2.description: Three-phase brushless DC motor controller with current sensing and over-temperature protection. Raino selected an approved architecture template and matched driver ICs to the torque requirements.

project3.title: USB-C PD Hub
project3.description: Multi-port USB-C power delivery hub with dynamic load balancing. Raino resolved ambiguities about port configurations and power profiles through the clarifying question loop.

project4.title: Precision Load Cell Interface
project4.description: High-resolution load cell amplifier with 24-bit ADC and digital filtering. Raino ingested the ADC datasheet and validated the analog front-end against noise and drift specifications.

project5.title: LoRaWAN Gateway Module
project5.description: Sub-GHz LoRa transceiver module with onboard antenna matching and FCC pre-certified RF layout. Raino matched the RF architecture template to the frequency band and output power requirements.

project6.title: Capacitive Touch Controller
project6.description: Multi-channel capacitive touch sensing board with haptic feedback driver. Raino generated the touch electrode layout and validated trace lengths against the controller datasheet.

hero.title: {accent}
hero.description: Version history and release notes. Track what is new, what changed, and what is coming next.

teamWorkspaces.title: Team Workspaces
teamWorkspaces.description: Collaborate on hardware projects with shared specs, review workflows, and role-based permissions.

customTemplates.title: Custom Architecture Templates
customTemplates.description: Upload and validate your own architecture templates for domain-specific design reuse.

live3dPreview.title: Live 3D Preview
live3dPreview.description: Interactive 3D board preview in the browser, powered by KiCad generated STEP files.

apiAccess.title: API Access
apiAccess.description: Programmatic access to the full workflow pipeline for CI/CD and automated design generation.

hero.title: Trust & {accent}
hero.description: Raino is built on transparency. Open source, honest fallbacks, and no surprises.

practices.title: Security {accent}
practices.description: Raino follows security best practices at every layer. From code to deployment, your data and designs are protected.

practice1.title: MIT Licensed
practice1.description: The entire Raino codebase is MIT licensed. You can inspect, fork, modify, and deploy your own instance without restrictions.

practice2.title: Open Source
practice2.description: Every line of code is on GitHub. There are no black boxes, no proprietary algorithms, and no hidden data processing.

practice3.title: No Tracking
practice3.description: Raino does not use analytics cookies, tracking pixels, or third-party telemetry. Your design data stays on your infrastructure.

practice4.title: Row-Level Security
practice4.description: Supabase RLS policies ensure users can only access their own data. Every database query is filtered by authenticated identity.

practice5.title: No Secrets in Code
practice5.description: API keys and credentials are never committed to the repository. Environment variables are validated at startup with clear error messages.

practice6.title: Audit Trail
practice6.description: Every design decision, part selection, and workflow step is logged. You can trace any output back to its inputs and rationale.

commitments.title: Our {accent}
commitments.description: These are not marketing claims. They are architectural constraints built into the system.

commitment1.title: No Fake Integrations
commitment1.description: Raino never fabricates live pricing, stock data, or API responses. When using fixture data, every value is clearly labeled as an estimate. You never have to guess whether the data you see is live or synthetic.

commitment2.title: No Silent Fallbacks
commitment2.description: When the system falls back to degraded mode, you are told explicitly. There are no silent downgrades, no hidden mock switches, and no pretense that fixture data comes from a live API.

commitment3.title: No Ghost Parts
commitment3.description: Every part number in a BOM traces back to a supplier datasheet, a catalog page, or a clearly labeled fixture estimate. There are no hallucinated components with made-up specifications.

commitment4.title: Human in the Loop
commitment4.description: Critical ambiguity stops the workflow. The system asks you before proceeding. You are the final authority on your own design, not the LLM.

dataCollection.title: Data Collection
dataCollection.description: Raino collects only the data necessary to run the hardware design workflow: your natural language descriptions, structured specifications, and generated design artifacts. We do not collect browsing history, device fingerprints, or behavioral analytics.

dataStorage.title: Data Storage
dataStorage.description: When you self-host Raino, all data stays on your Supabase instance. When using a hosted instance, data is stored in your dedicated project with RLS policies preventing cross-tenant access. We do not share data with third parties.

llmData.title: LLM Data Handling
llmData.description: Natural language descriptions are sent to the Kimi K2.5 API for parsing and reasoning. Raino does not send LLM providers any personally identifiable information beyond what you type into the intake form. Structured output is validated before storage.

yourRights.title: Your Rights
yourRights.description: You can export, modify, or delete your data at any time through the studio interface. Because Raino is open source, you can also run a complete data purge directly against your database.

openSource.title: Open {accent}
openSource.description: Trust is verified, not claimed. The entire Raino codebase is available on GitHub for inspection, audit, and contribution.
